speaker: BELARIUS
text: A goodly day not to keep house with such

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Whose roof’s as low as ours! Stoop, boys. This gate

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Instructs you how t’ adore the heavens and bows you

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To a morning’s holy office. The gates of monarchs

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Are arched so high that giants may jet through

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And keep their impious turbans on, without

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Good morrow to the sun. Hail, thou fair heaven!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: We house i’ th’ rock, yet use thee not so hardly

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As prouder livers do.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Hail, heaven!

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Hail, heaven!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Now for our mountain sport. Up to yond hill;

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Your legs are young. I’ll tread these flats. Consider,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: When you above perceive me like a crow,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That it is place which lessens and sets off,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And you may then revolve what tales I have told you

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Of courts, of princes, of the tricks in war.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This service is not service, so being done,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: But being so allowed. To apprehend thus

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Draws us a profit from all things we see,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And often, to our comfort, shall we find

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The sharded beetle in a safer hold

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Than is the full-winged eagle. O, this life

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Is nobler than attending for a check,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Richer than doing nothing for a robe,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Prouder than rustling in unpaid-for silk:

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Such gain the cap of him that makes him fine

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Yet keeps his book uncrossed. No life to ours.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Out of your proof you speak. We poor unfledged

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Have never winged from view o’ th’ nest, nor know not

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: What air ’s from home. Haply this life is best

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: If quiet life be best, sweeter to you

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: That have a sharper known, well corresponding

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: With your stiff age; but unto us it is

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: A cell of ignorance, traveling abed,

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: A prison for a debtor that not dares

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: To stride a limit.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: What should we speak of

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: When we are old as you? When we shall hear

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The rain and wind beat dark December, how

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: In this our pinching cave shall we discourse

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: The freezing hours away? We have seen nothing.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: We are beastly: subtle as the fox for prey,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Like warlike as the wolf for what we eat.

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: Our valor is to chase what flies. Our cage

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: We make a choir, as doth the prisoned bird,

speaker: ARVIRAGUS
text: And sing our bondage freely.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: How you speak!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Did you but know the city’s usuries

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And felt them knowingly; the art o’ th’ court,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As hard to leave as keep, whose top to climb

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Is certain falling, or so slipp’ry that

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The fear’s as bad as falling; the toil o’ th’ war,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: A pain that only seems to seek out danger

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I’ th’ name of fame and honor, which dies i’ th’ search

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And hath as oft a sland’rous epitaph

speaker: BELARIUS
text: As record of fair act—nay, many times

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Doth ill deserve by doing well; what’s worse,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Must curtsy at the censure. O boys, this story

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The world may read in me. My body’s marked

speaker: BELARIUS
text: With Roman swords, and my report was once

speaker: BELARIUS
text: First with the best of note. Cymbeline loved me,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And when a soldier was the theme, my name

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Was not far off. Then was I as a tree

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Whose boughs did bend with fruit. But in one night

speaker: BELARIUS
text: A storm or robbery, call it what you will,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Shook down my mellow hangings, nay, my leaves,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And left me bare to weather.

speaker: GUIDERIUS
text: Uncertain favor!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: My fault being nothing, as I have told you oft,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: But that two villains, whose false oaths prevailed

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Before my perfect honor, swore to Cymbeline

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I was confederate with the Romans. So

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Followed my banishment; and this twenty years

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This rock and these demesnes have been my world,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Where I have lived at honest freedom, paid

speaker: BELARIUS
text: More pious debts to heaven than in all

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The fore-end of my time. But up to th’ mountains!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: This is not hunters’ language. He that strikes

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The venison first shall be the lord o’ th’ feast;

speaker: BELARIUS
text: To him the other two shall minister,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And we will fear no poison, which attends

speaker: BELARIUS
text: In place of greater state. I’ll meet you in the valleys.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: How hard it is to hide the sparks of nature!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: These boys know little they are sons to th’ King,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Nor Cymbeline dreams that they are alive.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: They think they are mine, and, though trained up thus meanly,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: I’ th’ cave wherein they bow, their thoughts do hit

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The roofs of palaces, and nature prompts them

speaker: BELARIUS
text: In simple and low things to prince it much

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Beyond the trick of others. This Polydor,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The heir of Cymbeline and Britain, who

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The King his father called Guiderius—Jove!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: When on my three-foot stool I sit and tell

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The warlike feats I have done, his spirits fly out

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Into my story; say “Thus mine enemy fell,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And thus I set my foot on ’s neck,” even then

speaker: BELARIUS
text: The princely blood flows in his cheek, he sweats,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Strains his young nerves, and puts himself in posture

speaker: BELARIUS
text: That acts my words. The younger brother, Cadwal,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Once Arviragus, in as like a figure

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Strikes life into my speech and shows much more

speaker: BELARIUS
text: His own conceiving. Hark, the game is roused!

speaker: BELARIUS
text: O Cymbeline, heaven and my conscience knows

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou didst unjustly banish me; whereon,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: At three and two years old I stole these babes,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thinking to bar thee of succession as

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou refts me of my lands. Euriphile,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Thou wast their nurse; they took thee for their mother,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: And every day do honor to her grave.

speaker: BELARIUS
text: Myself, Belarius, that am Morgan called,

speaker: BELARIUS
text: They take for natural father. The game is up!
